title: OpenClaw Heartbeat — Proactive AI without the noise
description: OpenClaw's heartbeat system lets AI agents run periodic check-ins, surface urgent info, and do background work — all without spamming you. Configurable active hours, isolated sessions, and light context mode keep token costs low.
actionNote: Already implemented this for Día! Running every 30min during your work hours.

title: Linear + AI = Automatic sprint planning that actually works
description: Teams are using Linear's API + AI agents to auto-prioritize backlogs based on customer impact signals. The pattern: ingest support tickets, map to Linear issues, auto-bump priority on frequently-hit bugs.
actionNote: Could build this for LawTrades — auto-prioritize based on client complaints hitting Slack.

title: r/ExperiencedDevs: The 'Chief of Staff AI' pattern is real
description: Devs on Reddit are sharing setups where AI agents act as chief of staff — pulling standup notes, triaging tickets, and prepping meeting briefs automatically. The key insight: AI is most useful when it has persistent context, not just one-shot prompts.
actionNote: This is exactly what we're building. Consider posting your setup when it's more mature.

title: Google DeepMind launches Gemma 4: Open AI models for advanced reasoning and agents
description: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4, its most capable open model family yet — designed for advanced reasoning and autonomous AI agent workflows. Available for both data centers and mobile devices. Byte-for-byte the most capable open models to date, per DeepMind.
actionNote: Worth testing Gemma 4 as a local/cheap reasoning layer for LawTrades AI tasks.

title: Agentic AI infrastructure is hitting legal: AUTOMATIC + Law.co partnership
description: AUTOMATIC (agentic AI infra) partnered with Law.co to deliver autonomous AI pipelines for law firms — automating intake, document review, and client workflows. Legal AI is moving fast from copilots to full agents. Direct competitive signal for LawTrades.
actionNote: Competitive intel — law firms are getting agentic AI. LawTrades should be the marketplace that connects this talent/tech to companies.
